svg.text: 15.2 · Lost transactions on crash — the durability window (M09/14.9)
how many CONFIRMED transfers vanish depends on the config — money = 1/1 + semi-sync closes the window
★ flush_log=1 + sync_binlog=1 (MONEY)
fsync EVERY commit before "committed" is returned
→ crash loses NOTHING (crash recovery replays redo, M09)
+ semi-sync (M10) → survives total PRIMARY loss too
flush_log=2 (write OS cache, fsync ~1s)
mysqld crash → OS still has it → no loss
OS/POWER crash → lose ~1s of confirmed commits
⚠ confirmed transfers VANISH (told "paid", then gone)
flush_log=0 / sync_binlog=0
fsync ~1s regardless → even a mysqld crash loses ~1s
⚠ + sync_binlog=0 → binlog gap (no PITR, 15.7)
promote-before-apply (M10/10.10)
async replica promoted before applying relay log
⚠ loses the un-applied transfers → choose the FRESHEST replica
WHY: durability is a chain — app → OS cache → disk cache → platter (M09/9.7). "durable" = fsync'd THROUGH to stable storage
a crash loses whatever was ACKNOWLEDGED but not yet FSYNC'd — the window size IS the config
💰 VERDICT: money LOST (confirmed transfers vanish) — UNLESS 1/1 + semi-sync (then nothing lost)
prevention: 1/1 + semi-sync (group commit amortizes the cost, M09/9.11) · detection: reconciliation vs external records (M12/12.14)
recovery: re-drive lost transactions idempotently from the external source-of-truth (M12/12.9)
